title: User IDs in Monday
excerpt: I am trying set up an action in Zapier in order to assign the item against a user it is requesting the user’s monday user ID number. Where can I find my teams user ID numbers?

posts.author: achidiac (Alex Chidiac)
posts.cookedHtml: I am trying set up an action in Zapier in order to assign the item against a user it is requesting the user’s monday user ID number. Where can I find my teams user ID numbers?

posts.author: florence023 (Florence Gayhart)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

To find your team members’ user ID numbers in Mondaycom, you can follow these steps:

Account ID (Recommended):
While there isn’t a straightforward way for end users to directly retrieve their account ID, you can use the account URL to identify an account.
Ask your team members to provide you with their account URL (the URL they use to access Mondaycom). The account ID is typically embedded in that URL.
Alternative Approach (User ID):
For user-specific actions, such as assigning items, you’ll need the user ID.
Unfortunately, there isn’t a built-in way for users to easily find their user ID within the Mondaycom interface.
You might need to refer them to the Mondaycom API playground or use other methods to retrieve their user IDs programmatically.
Remember, using the account URL (account ID) is often more straightforward for identifying accounts. If you specifically need user IDs, consider exploring the API or other integrations to retrieve this information.

Hope this will help you.
Best regards,
florence023

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alex,
I believe Make has a “List users” action which should help you get a list of all the users in your organization with their respective IDs.
